title: Mike Levine
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Mike Levine
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
biography: Mike Levine is an assistant professor of musicology at Christopher Newport University. His research utilizes ethnographic fieldwork conducted in Havana, Miami, and through digital platforms to investigate “el paquete semanal” (a popular USB-based internet platform in Cuba) and its relationship to improvised modes of informal music sharing and Cuban reparto music. He has written for "Sounding Out!", Hypermedia , Cuban Studies , Twentieth Century Music , and the volume Independence in 21st-Century Popular Music . He is currently drafting a monograph, Pegao: Underground Music and the Rise of Reparto in Post-Socialist Cuba , for University Press of Florida.
education_entries: Ph D in Musicology, University of North Carolina - Chapel Hill
MA in Musicology, City of University of New York - Brooklyn College
MA in Music Technology, New York University - Steinhardt School of Education
research_interests: Cuban Music / Internet Studies / Popular Music
teaching_interests: Cuban Music / Internet Music / Popular Music / Caribbean and Latin American Popular Musics

Artistic Accomplishments: Music Performance - Participation "Music and Neuroscience: An Exploration of Sound and the Brain". Empire State University and Christopher Newport University. Scholes Street Studio. Brooklyn, NY.
